evals.prompt: Quero avaliar a ideia de criar um produto SaaS de diagnóstico de IA para PMEs portuguesas. Faz sentido?
evals.expected_output: A structured initiative evaluation memo with market sizing, viability sketch, evidence-based SWOT, competitive landscape, and a pursue/drop/investigate recommendation with the most decision-relevant unknown.
expectations: The skill calls kb_search for the company profile, prior related initiatives, and relevant frameworks before any analysis.
The market section quantifies demand (who pays, how much, how often) rather than stating that a market exists.
The SWOT has a maximum of 4 bullets per quadrant, all evidence-based — no filler bullets.
The competition section names direct and indirect alternatives and states WizardingCode's differentiation.
The memo ends with exactly 'Recommendation: pursue / drop / investigate' plus the single most decision-relevant unknown.

evals.prompt: We're considering a partnership with a Portuguese accounting firm to co-sell AI automation services to their clients. Should we pursue it?
evals.expected_output: An initiative evaluation memo for the accounting-firm partnership covering market, viability, SWOT, and competition, ready to feed into the CEO balanced-decision gate.
expectations: The skill runs a KB-first lookup for prior partnership evaluations and the Dream 100 / growth context in the KB.
The viability section includes an effort/cost estimate and a unit-economics sketch (revenue per unit vs. fully-loaded cost for the partnership model).
The SWOT Threats quadrant explicitly addresses competitor partnerships or alternative channels the accounting firm could use instead.
The memo identifies the capability fit: which WizardingCode departments must execute this (Growth, COO) and whether the capacity exists.
The memo is ingested via kb_ingest with source 'memos/YYYY-MM-DD-initiative-eval-<slug>.md'.

evals.prompt: Avalia a hipótese de lançar um programa de formação online em automação com IA, direcionado a gestores de PME.
evals.expected_output: An initiative evaluation memo for the AI training programme with market, viability (including competition from existing courses), SWOT, and a pursue/drop/investigate recommendation.
expectations: The skill notes prior KB context about content/education initiatives at WizardingCode before analysing.
The market section addresses demand signals specifically for AI training targeted at Portuguese SME managers — not generic e-learning market data.
The competition section names at least one existing Portuguese or EU-based AI training competitor or programme.
Every SWOT bullet is evidence-based, citing either KB sources or explicit market observations.
The recommendation is exactly one of pursue / drop / investigate, followed by the single most decision-relevant unknown.
